diffCoverage.notes: Focus on the spawned bridge task, channel backpressure, and listener handle lifetime.

findings.evidence: Both bridge tasks exit cleanly on a dropped receiver: the zalo->core forwarder breaks when out.send errors, and the core->frontend forwarder breaks when app.emit errors. The Listener handle is retained in ListenerState so the socket is not dropped immediately after start_listening returns. Reconnect/backoff is handled inside zca-rust (start(true) with retry_on_close).
findings.fix: Add an explicit stop/teardown command and per-account handle tracking when session-manager lands (multi-account lifecycle).

findings.evidence: Bounded mpsc channels (256/16) provide backpressure; the bridge does not use unbounded buffering or a busy-loop. The live smoke bounds its wait with a 20s timeout rather than blocking forever.
findings.fix: Surface Disconnected/Closed listener events to the UI so a dropped socket is visible; track as a follow-up for session-manager.

summary: Reliability review of the spawned bridge: tasks terminate on receiver drop, channels are bounded, the listener handle is kept alive in managed state, and reconnect is delegated to zca-rust's retry loop. Known follow-ups (explicit teardown, surfacing disconnect to UI) are non-blocking and belong to session-manager. No blocking findings.
